tasks.description: Translate a simple informational paragraph from English to Spanish
tasks.input:
Translate the following into Spanish:

"The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles across northern China. Built over many centuries, it served as a military defense system against invasions from the north. Today, it is one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, attracting millions of visitors each year. Several sections have been restored for public access, with Badaling being the most popular among international tourists."

scoring.5: All facts preserved: distance (converted to km or miles with note), historical purpose, tourism status, Badaling reference; no meaning errors
scoring.3: Core meaning preserved but minor inaccuracies or omissions
scoring.1: Several factual errors or significant omissions

scoring.5: Reads as natural Spanish prose; proper grammar, appropriate connectors, no anglicisms
scoring.3: Readable but with some unnatural constructions
scoring.1: Clearly translated; awkward phrasing throughout

scoring.5: Correct Spanish punctuation, number formatting, proper noun handling (La Gran Muralla China); appropriate register for informational text
scoring.3: Most conventions followed with minor deviations
scoring.1: English conventions imposed on Spanish text

tasks.description: Translate a short email from English to French with appropriate register
tasks.input:
Translate this business email into French. Maintain the formal professional register.

"Dear Mr. Laurent,

Thank you for your prompt response regarding the Q3 budget proposal. We have reviewed the figures and would like to schedule a meeting to discuss the allocation for the marketing department. Would Thursday at 2:00 PM work for your team?

Please let us know at your earliest convenience.

Best regards,
Sarah Chen
Director of Operations"

scoring.5: All content preserved: greeting, gratitude, meeting request, time, closing, name and title; no omissions
scoring.3: Core message preserved but minor details lost
scoring.1: Key information missing (time, purpose, or context)

scoring.5: Proper French business register: vous-form throughout, formal salutation (Cher Monsieur Laurent), appropriate closing (Cordialement / Bien cordialement), professional tone
scoring.3: Generally formal but some register inconsistencies
scoring.1: Informal tone inappropriate for business correspondence

scoring.5: Reads as a native French business email; natural phrasing, proper French epistolary conventions
scoring.3: Readable but with some anglicisms or unnatural constructions
scoring.1: Clearly translated from English

tasks.description: Translate UI strings from English to German with length awareness
tasks.input:
Translate these UI strings into German. These are for button labels and menu items, so brevity is important. Flag any translations that are significantly longer than the English original.

1. "Submit"
2. "Cancel"
3. "Settings"
4. "Log out"
5. "Save changes"
6. "Delete account"
7. "Undo"
8. "Share with team"

scoring.5: All 8 strings correctly translated with standard German UI conventions (e.g., Absenden, Abbrechen, Einstellungen, Abmelden, etc.)
scoring.3: Most strings correct but 1-2 non-standard choices
scoring.1: Several incorrect or overly literal translations

scoring.5: Flags strings with significant expansion (e.g., 'Share with team' -> 'Mit Team teilen'); offers shorter alternatives where possible; notes expansion percentages
scoring.3: Acknowledges length differences but no detailed analysis
scoring.1: No awareness of length constraints
scoring.0: Produces excessively long translations without comment

scoring.5: Uses standard German software UI terminology consistent with major platforms (Windows/macOS/web); correct capitalization per German convention
scoring.3: Mostly standard terminology with minor deviations
scoring.1: Non-standard or inconsistent with established UI conventions

tasks.description: Translate a legal clause from English to Chinese with formal equivalence
tasks.input:
Translate this legal clause into Simplified Chinese. This is from a commercial contract and must preserve precise legal meaning. Use formal equivalence approach.

"Limitation of Liability. In no event shall either party be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, data, or business opportunities, arising out of or related to this Agreement, regardless of the theory of liability (contract, tort, strict liability, or otherwise), even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. Each party's total cumulative liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid by Client to Provider during the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to such liability."

scoring.5: All legal concepts precisely rendered: each damage type (indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive) has the correct Chinese legal equivalent; liability theories correctly translated; limitation cap and time period accurate
scoring.3: Core liability limitation conveyed but some legal terms imprecise or conflated
scoring.1: Several legal concepts incorrectly translated; meaning altered

scoring.5: Consistent rendering of repeated terms (party/当事人, Agreement/本协议, liability/责任); damage types each have distinct, correct translations maintained throughout
scoring.3: Mostly consistent with 1-2 variations
scoring.1: Inconsistent terminology causing confusion

scoring.5: Reads as formal Chinese legal prose; uses established Chinese contract language conventions (在任何情况下, 不应超过, 前述); proper legal document structure
scoring.3: Generally formal but some non-legal phrasing
scoring.1: Casual or non-legal tone in a legal document

scoring.5: Every clause, qualifier, and condition translated; no omissions; parenthetical (12) preserved; enumeration complete
scoring.3: Minor omissions that do not affect core meaning
scoring.1: Significant content omitted

tasks.description: Translate marketing copy from English to Japanese with cultural adaptation
tasks.input:
Translate this marketing email into Japanese for a Japanese consumer audience. Adapt cultural references and tone for the target market while preserving the promotional intent.

"Hey there, coffee lover! ☕

Summer's here, and we've got something cool for you — literally! Our new Cold Brew Collection just dropped, and it's a game-changer. Think smooth, bold flavors with zero bitterness.

🔥 What's hot (or should we say, cold?):
- Midnight Mocha: Rich, chocolatey, and dangerously addictive
- Vanilla Sunset: Sweet, creamy, and perfect for those lazy afternoon vibes
- Citrus Spark: A zesty twist that'll wake you right up

Use code CHILLOUT25 for 25% off your first order. But hurry — this deal melts faster than ice on a July sidewalk!

Stay cool,
The Bean Team"

scoring.5: Successfully adapts American casual marketing tone to Japanese consumer conventions; handles idioms ('game-changer', 'dropped', 'vibes') with natural Japanese equivalents; adjusts humor style; adapts seasonal references appropriately
scoring.3: Some cultural adaptation but retains several Americanisms that feel foreign in Japanese
scoring.1: Minimal adaptation; reads as a literal translation of American copy
scoring.0: No cultural awareness; culturally inappropriate content

scoring.5: Preserves urgency, excitement, and call-to-action; promo code and discount clearly conveyed; product descriptions enticing in Japanese context
scoring.3: Core promotional elements present but some urgency or excitement lost
scoring.1: Promotional intent weakened; reads more informational than persuasive

scoring.5: Uses appropriate Japanese casual-polite register for consumer marketing (ですます with friendly tone); emoji and formatting adapted to Japanese conventions
scoring.3: Register mostly appropriate but some inconsistencies (too formal or too casual in places)
scoring.1: Register mismatch — overly formal business Japanese or inappropriately casual

scoring.5: All product names, descriptions, promo code, discount amount, and call-to-action preserved; no content omitted
scoring.3: Minor details missing but core message complete
scoring.1: Significant content omitted

tasks.description: Translate a medical information leaflet from English to Arabic with domain terminology
tasks.input:
Translate this patient information leaflet into Modern Standard Arabic. Maintain medical accuracy while ensuring the text is accessible to a general patient audience.

"PATIENT INFORMATION: Metformin Hydrochloride Tablets

What is Metformin?
Metformin is an oral antidiabetic medication used to treat type 2 diabetes mellitus. It works by decreasing glucose production in the liver and improving insulin sensitivity in muscle tissue.

Dosage:
- Starting dose: 500 mg twice daily with meals
- Maximum dose: 2,550 mg per day, divided into three doses
- Swallow tablets whole with a glass of water

Common Side Effects:
- Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (usually improve after 1-2 weeks)
- Metallic taste in the mouth
- Loss of appetite

WARNING: Seek immediate medical attention if you experience symptoms of lactic acidosis: unusual muscle pain, difficulty breathing, stomach pain with nausea, feeling cold, or dizziness."

scoring.5: All medical terms correctly rendered in MSA medical terminology (ميتفورمين, السكري من النوع الثاني, الحماض اللبني, etc.); dosages precise; mechanism of action accurately conveyed
scoring.3: Core medical content accurate but some terms imprecise or using non-standard equivalents
scoring.1: Medical inaccuracies that could mislead patients

scoring.5: Technical terms explained at patient level; clear instructions; warning section prominent and unambiguous; reads naturally for Arabic-speaking patients
scoring.3: Mostly accessible but some overly technical phrasing for a patient audience
scoring.1: Too technical or too simplified; unclear instructions

scoring.5: Preserves document structure (sections, bullet points, warning); correct RTL formatting considerations; Arabic punctuation and number conventions followed
scoring.3: Structure mostly preserved with minor formatting issues
scoring.1: Structure lost or significantly altered

scoring.5: Every section, dosage, side effect, and warning item translated; no omissions; all numbers and units preserved
scoring.3: Minor omissions that do not affect patient safety
scoring.1: Significant content missing, especially safety information

tasks.description: Translate a technical document with placeholders and code from English to Korean
tasks.input:
Translate this API documentation excerpt into Korean. Preserve all code examples, variable names, and placeholders exactly as they appear. Translate only the natural-language descriptions.

"## Authentication

All API requests require a valid access token. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}
```

### Getting a Token

Send a POST request to `/api/v2/auth/token` with your credentials:

```json
{
  "client_id": "your_client_id",
  "client_secret": "your_client_secret",
  "grant_type": "client_credentials"
}
```

**Response:** Returns a JSON object containing `access_token` (string), `expires_in` (integer, seconds), and `token_type` (always `"Bearer"`).

**Note:** Tokens expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour). Implement token refresh logic to avoid service interruptions."

scoring.5: All code blocks, variable names ({access_token}, client_id, etc.), API paths (/api/v2/auth/token), inline code (`Authorization`, `Bearer`), and JSON structure preserved exactly; only natural-language text translated
scoring.3: Most code preserved but 1-2 elements accidentally translated or modified
scoring.1: Multiple code elements translated or broken

scoring.5: Natural-language descriptions accurately and naturally translated into Korean; technical concepts correctly conveyed; reads as native Korean developer documentation
scoring.3: Translations accurate but somewhat stiff or unnatural
scoring.1: Translation errors or unnatural phrasing that hinders comprehension

scoring.5: Standard Korean IT terminology used (인증, 액세스 토큰, 요청, 응답, etc.); consistent with established Korean developer documentation conventions
scoring.3: Mostly standard terminology with some non-standard choices
scoring.1: Non-standard or inconsistent technical terminology

scoring.5: Markdown structure preserved (headings, code blocks, bold, inline code); document layout maintained; proper Korean punctuation
scoring.3: Most formatting preserved with minor issues
scoring.1: Significant formatting loss

tasks.description: Translate a literary passage with idioms, metaphors, and cultural references from English to Chinese
tasks.input:
Translate this literary passage into Simplified Chinese. Preserve the literary style, adapt idioms and cultural references for a Chinese reader, and maintain the emotional tone. Provide translator notes for any significant adaptation choices.

"The old lighthouse keeper had seen better days — much like the lighthouse itself, whose paint peeled in long strips like sunburned skin. He was a man of few words, the kind who let silences speak volumes and whose eyes told stories his lips never would.

'You can't fight the tide,' he'd say, whenever some young hotshot from the city tried to tell him about progress. 'The sea doesn't care about your five-year plans.'

He kept his world small and his coffee strong. Every morning, rain or shine, he'd climb the 127 steps to the top — his knees singing their familiar protest song — and check the light. It was a Sisyphean task in many ways: the salt air corroded everything it touched, and yesterday's maintenance was always undone by tomorrow's storm.

But there was a certain dignity in it, he thought. A man ought to have something worth climbing for."

scoring.5: Translation reads as accomplished Chinese literary prose; metaphors effectively recreated (peeling paint/sunburned skin, silence speaking, knees singing); narrative voice preserved with appropriate Chinese literary devices
scoring.3: Readable Chinese but literary quality diminished; some metaphors flattened
scoring.1: Flat, prosaic translation that loses literary character

scoring.5: Sisyphean reference handled skillfully (explained, adapted to Chinese mythological equivalent, or glossed); idioms like 'seen better days', 'hotshot', 'rain or shine' rendered with natural Chinese equivalents; cultural context maintained
scoring.3: Some idioms adapted but others translated too literally; Sisyphean reference present but awkward
scoring.1: Most cultural references lost or confusing for Chinese readers

scoring.5: Captures the melancholy dignity, quiet humor, and philosophical undertone of the original; the ending line resonates with the same weight in Chinese
scoring.3: General mood preserved but some emotional nuances lost
scoring.1: Emotional tone significantly altered (too dramatic, too flat, or wrong mood)

scoring.5: Provides thoughtful translator notes on key adaptation choices (Sisyphean reference, idiom substitutions, literary device handling); notes demonstrate translation reasoning
scoring.3: Some translator notes but lacking depth or missing key decisions
scoring.1: Minimal or no translator notes despite significant adaptations

tasks.description: Translate an ambiguous multi-register document from Chinese to English with disambiguation
tasks.input:
Translate the following Chinese text into English. The text contains formal legal language, technical terminology, and a quoted informal conversation. Maintain the register distinctions in your English translation. Flag any ambiguities you encounter.

"根据《中华人民共和国数据安全法》第二十七条之规定，数据处理者应当建立健全数据安全管理制度。本公司于2025年1月15日召开了全体员工数据安全培训会议。

会议期间，技术总监王明指出：'我们的API网关日均处理请求量已达到200万次，必须实施零信任架构来保障数据传输安全。目前的TLS 1.2配置需要升级到1.3版本，同时要对所有微服务间的东西向流量实施mTLS双向认证。'

会后，几位工程师在茶水间的对话：
小李：'老王今天讲的那个零信任，说实话我觉得有点悬。咱们那破服务器扛得住吗？'
小张：'可不是嘛，上次升级TLS差点没把生产环境搞崩。不过老板都发话了，硬着头皮也得上啊。'
小李：'行吧，那咱俩下周一块儿搞？'
小张：'没问题，到时候叫上老赵，他对网络这块儿比较熟。'"

scoring.5: Three distinct registers clearly rendered: formal legal/regulatory language for the opening, professional technical language for the CTO's statement, casual colloquial English for the engineer chat; register transitions are natural
scoring.3: Some register distinction but not consistently maintained; casual section too formal or formal section too casual
scoring.1: Uniform register throughout; no distinction between the three sections

scoring.5: All technical terms correctly rendered (zero trust architecture, API gateway, TLS 1.2/1.3, mTLS, east-west traffic, microservices); legal citation format appropriate; numbers and dates accurate
scoring.3: Most technical terms correct but some imprecise
scoring.1: Several technical errors

scoring.5: Identifies and annotates ambiguities: informal nicknames (小李/小张/老王/老赵) translated with appropriate English conventions and notes; cultural context of workplace hierarchy explained; colloquialisms flagged where English equivalents are approximate
scoring.3: Some ambiguities noted but others missed
scoring.1: Ambiguities silently resolved without annotation

scoring.5: Each section reads naturally in English: legal section sounds like a compliance document, technical section like a CTO briefing, casual section like authentic workplace banter
scoring.3: Generally fluent but some sections feel translated
scoring.1: Stilted or unnatural throughout

tasks.description: Translate a long multi-paragraph document from English to Portuguese (Brazil) with terminology consistency across the full document
tasks.input:
Translate this software release notes document into Brazilian Portuguese. This is a public-facing document for end users. Maintain consistent terminology for all recurring technical terms throughout the entire translation. Provide a glossary of key terms used.

"Release Notes — Version 4.2.0

New Features:
1. Real-time Collaboration: Multiple users can now edit the same document simultaneously. Changes sync instantly across all connected clients through our new WebSocket-based event system. Conflict resolution uses an operational transformation algorithm to merge concurrent edits seamlessly.

2. Advanced Search: The search engine has been completely rebuilt. It now supports fuzzy matching, boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT), field-specific queries (author:name, date:range), and real-time search suggestions powered by our new inverted index.

3. Custom Dashboards: Create personalized dashboards with drag-and-drop widgets. Each widget connects to a data source and updates in real-time. Supported widget types include: charts, tables, KPI cards, and embedded reports.

Improvements:
- The document editor now loads 40% faster thanks to lazy loading of non-critical components
- Search results now display highlighted snippets showing where your query matched
- Dashboard widgets can be resized and repositioned without refreshing the page
- The collaboration cursor now shows each user's name and a unique color identifier

Bug Fixes:
- Fixed an issue where the search index would not update after bulk document imports
- Resolved a race condition in the collaboration engine that occasionally caused duplicate content
- Fixed dashboard widget data not refreshing when the underlying data source was modified
- Corrected a display issue where search result snippets truncated mid-word on mobile devices

Known Issues:
- Real-time collaboration is limited to 25 concurrent users per document (will be increased in v4.3)
- Custom dashboard layouts are not yet supported on tablet devices
- The advanced search boolean NOT operator may return unexpected results when combined with fuzzy matching"

scoring.5: Every recurring term translated identically throughout: 'dashboard/painel' (or chosen equivalent), 'widget', 'search/pesquisa/busca' (consistently one), 'collaboration/colaboração', 'real-time/tempo real', 'data source/fonte de dados', etc.; glossary provided with all key terms
scoring.3: Mostly consistent but 2-3 terms vary across sections
scoring.1: Frequent inconsistencies; same concept has different translations in different sections

scoring.5: Reads as natural Brazilian Portuguese (not European Portuguese); appropriate informal-professional register for public release notes; no anglicisms where Portuguese alternatives exist
scoring.3: Generally good quality but some anglicisms or European Portuguese forms
scoring.1: Stiff or unnatural; clearly translated

scoring.5: All technical concepts accurately conveyed: WebSocket, operational transformation, inverted index, lazy loading, race condition, boolean operators; appropriate decisions on which terms to translate vs. keep in English
scoring.3: Most technical content accurate but some concepts imprecisely rendered
scoring.1: Several technical errors or confusing translations of technical concepts

scoring.5: All sections fully translated (features, improvements, bug fixes, known issues); no omissions; comprehensive glossary with 10+ terms including rationale for key decisions
scoring.3: All sections translated; glossary present but incomplete
scoring.1: Some content missing or no glossary
